action.reasoning: Observation: Iter_11 (30M steps, ent_coef=0.03) scored 49.4% success, slightly below the best 49.6% from iter_8 (15M steps, ent_coef=0.01). Four environments remain at 0% (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom) and LavaCrossing regressed from 90% to 86%, indicating excessive exploration entropy. Analysis: The policy has plateaued because hard tasks get too few samples and too little gradient signal in a uniform 10-env mix, while high entropy hurts already-solved tasks. Decision: 1) Oversample hard envs (3x copies of KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom; 2x FourRooms) to increase their training signal. 2) Revert entropy to 0.01 for better exploitation. 3) Increase capacity to features_dim=1024 and net_arch=[1024,512] (still well under 10M params). 4) Raise n_epochs to 10 for better sample efficiency and lower clip_range to 0.1 for stability. 5) Keep n_steps=4096 for fast updates. 6) Train for 10M timesteps (~1.5h expected), fitting safely within the 2.6h budget and leaving time for eval.
